item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's because it was part of the SCP 3000 Contest!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good article, though I gotta say I'm curious as to why a Series III article is tagged with 3000 🤔

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was cool. I didn't see the dust bowl coming until the end, but still, really really good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! For my first article on the site, I think I did relatively well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kinda reminds of the ending of The Witcher 3. Well done though. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's actually a reference to a skip who "jumps" between dimensions and voluntarily works with the foundation. One world he repeatedly "jumps" to is a cave with light that hurts you and a man with a wide grin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Speaking of interesting cross links, there is a scp that is the indestructible skin of a young man whose skin cells create dust that causes mesothelioma like asbestos. Were it to build up (and you can't get rid of it) and animate you might get a situation like this.

Edit for spelling and to add:

This one I was thinking of is http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-1636

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the inclusion of different versions of the house made this better. I love to imagain the different versions of the house.

1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sounds a little too on the nose :p

I'm sticking to From Dust to Dawn for now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That made my day, thank you :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Top contender here.

Dust and Echoes

A Dusty House

Dust to Dust to Dust to

Razed

A World of Dust

Left but Dust

Black Blizzard (referencing The Dust Bowl, look it up)

The Door to Dust

From Dust to Dawn/From Dawn to Dust (This is it for now)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What I read was a great SCP, or almost a doing of art.

That gave me the goosebumps of reading this, Jesus. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How about 'Dust and Echoes'?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How about "Dust Is Comprised Of The Remains Of Human Tissue" as a title?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Woah woah woah.

Let me get this straight.

I pick a random number, 2853 and call it Dust to Dust

2583 is also Dust to Dust? Wtf.

Welp, guess I got to think of a new name for a horror that grinds people into dust

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice SCP, but I'd like to note that SCP-2583 is already called "Dust to Dust". Might be interesting to set up a cross-link, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are a few reasons why I downvoted:

The first is a sort of a personal pet peeve, but I'm not really feeling the "zero information before the logs" pacing of this story. The reason this annoys me is because it never makes sense from an in-universe perspective: the things that are obvious from the exploration logs (room shifts each time you enter, abnormal amounts of hostile dust) would be stated explicitly in the description. But usually, people have a good narrative reason for not revealing information that the Foundation would probably immediately present. Here, I don't see a particularly compelling narrative reason why I don't just get the important info straight off the bat, because the important info (endless substance what wants to kill you, shifting dimensions) is pretty boiler-plate.

The second kind of segues from the pacing issue: the exploration logs didn't really do it for me. Two of them were basically the same; you could've skipped all of the first if you'd just said that there was a lot of dust in the place and used that to justify the air filtration system. By the time I'm in the third one, I'm thinking, okay, when is this person going to get killed by the dust. The fact that I'm in these exploration logs to learn information I feel like I should've already been told, along with the somewhat-predictable sequence of events in each one, didn't really do it for me.

Finally, the overall concept doesn't resonate. The idea of getting killed by an unending wave of dust is no more or less scary than the idea of getting killed by an unending wave of any substance. The idea of some supernatural force destroying parallel universe after parallel universe is just the base of a storyline at this point, and the article didn't make me feel anything new about the idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeaah… I got that one too. Damn old man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What do you suggest I do about the final documents? You aren't the first to say they detract a bit

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this version a lot. The final documents are still tipping your hand a lot, but there's enough variety in the things discussed there and what went down in the third log that there are a lot more scenarios to run through in my head, which is a plus.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll look into some other logs to see the formatting. TBH I've seen it presented in multiple ways if I remember correctly

Access is restricted because of introduction of new entity following the recovery of the second log's documents.

Third log implies that whatever is inside could quite possibly be the vector for our own demise. If it doesn't seem appropriate, I can remove the collapsible

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this a lot as a non-3000 entry! The new (?) third log is probably the most interesting, but I'm not sure why access is restricted in-universe. Also, I do think it's a little weird how the dialogue in the exploration logs has quotes around it. It usually doesn't, IIRC.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree. While the writing is the most important aspect, that picture pretty much matched my mental layout of the house, even the dim, cloudy windows and accurate furniture placement.

I'll keep looking.

Maybe I'll just have to wait until the dust gets that bad in my own apartment. Well on its way

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd love it if you found another picture for this; Skips with pictures tend to just look more document-ey.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Sounds like too much a hassle, I guess we can remove it!

Stinks though cause it eerily matches my mental picture of the skip

Indeed. This tends to happen for some older articles.

Removing image code.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I went to re-read it to find some specific examples, and it seemed fine the second time around. I might have just been imagining it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good advice! Changing that now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, this is well improved since the last time I saw it. Some comments:

a family of four (4)

This is a really silly spot to do this. There's also an 'inch' measurement in that first log.

The bit about the gun is very prosaic, probably should be taken out or tone-mown. I'd also take out "like a cannon" (maybe give an estimated dB?) The globe not accurately representing Earth is good shit, though. :D Also, it's not immediately obvious the last two collapsibles are indeed separate, you might want to put a return in there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, I suppose. I wanted to add a little black humor that still could pass as an actual report.

I'll take it into account though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The gun seemed ineffective in stopping their demise.

This sentence seems both non-clinical and unnecessary.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Unless this technically is revenue generating.

Sounds like too much a hassle, I guess we can remove it!

Stinks though cause it eerily matches my mental picture of the skip

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Fair enough, I was reading into what they allow and do not allow in terms of usage of said licensed content:

You may not (i) deep link or employ software or any automatic device, technology or algorithm, to "crawl", "scrape", search or monitor this Site and/or retrieve or copy Content or related information; (ii) violate the mechanical restrictions of this Site, or bypass other measures employed to prevent or limit access to this Site or Site Elements by hacking or other means; (iii) copy, redirect, or exploit this Site or any Site Elements; (iv) probe, scan, or test the vulnerability of this Site or of the network supporting this Site, or seek information on visitors to this Site or personal information of AP’s customers; (v) use or upload to this Site any device, software or routine that would interfere with the proper functioning of this Site, or any transaction conducted via this Site; (vi) copy, modify, or display AP’s name, logo, trademarks or service marks, text, or graphic images in any way, or include AP’s names or trademarks or service marks or any AP executive's name, or any variation of any of the foregoing, as a meta-tag, hidden textual element, or any other indicator that may create an impression of affiliation, sponsorship, or endorsement; (vii) use devices (including software) that are designed to provide repeated automated access to this Site, other than those made generally available by AP, or probe, scan, or test the vulnerability of any system or network related in any way to this Site, without proper authorization from or by AP; (viii) engage in any unlawful or illegal activities in connection with the use of this Site; or (ix) frame this Site or Site Elements using "framing" or any other technology or process which allows a page from a different Internet URL to call a page containing the Images and to display the Images within the calling page.

None of those criteria fit my usage, I believe

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Reading AP's terms of use, I noted this:

All of the content elements displayed and/or downloadable on this Site, including photographic images, graphic images… (together with the Content, collectively, the "Site Elements") is the exclusive property of AP or its licensors.

And digging even deeper into AP's Image License Terms, I noted this:

AP and its licensors reserve all rights in and to the Content other than those rights that have been expressly granted to You in a License Agreement or these License Terms.

Thus, it is within my current opinion that AP is holding the copyright. Based on the terms of use, there is no ruling that images can be used without modification. If you identify such a ruling, please quote it explicitly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks, yeah I agree.

I tried to give them different personalities, add a little nervous humor, etc

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image is actually from the Merapi volcano eruption!

This article about it here: https://svet.sme.sk/c/5613764/sopka-merapi-si-prisla-po-svojho-strazcu.html sources the image from the associated press.

AP's image policy seems pretty lax

http://www.apimages.com/TermsOfUse

As long as I do not modify the image, I'm good to go

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Actually and more importantly, I would need the license of the image used. As in, if it were Creative Commons or Public Domain, please state accordingly. If it were a copyrighted image, please obtain permission from the copyright holder. If you cannot explicitly prove it to be Creative Commons or Public Domain or equivalent, assume it is copyrighted by default.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some of the D-class dialogue felt a little off,

There's quite a bit of dialogue in this, could you be more specific in what you mean? Where does it feel off, and/or in what way? This is too vague to be helpful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's all for you <3

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Image was first acquired from https://www.pinterest.com/pin/188447565634542283/

where it was taken from http://hitrostigizni.ru/4-sposoba-sokratit-kolichestvo-pyli-v-dome/

where it was taken from http://trickss.ru/wp-content/uploads/2013/06/4-sposoba-sokratit-kolichestvo-py-li-v-dome.jpg

where it was sourced to http://funnyoldplanet.com/, which no longer exists.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love exploration logs, and these were no exception. Very well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am back so soon. (Glad you got the reference) 'Ve been working on four skips but this one is my baby.

I'll take another look at the dialogue, thanks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ spoilers
– hide block

Back so soon?

Some of the D-class dialogue felt a little off, but this was really good otherwise. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah! I wanted to throw that in there just to creep out anyone who gets that reference.

You say the exploration logs are too numerous? Do you think the third log does not add anything to the article? Thanks for the input!

Man, you must hate 093 and 610

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed this skip. The exploration logs were too numerous for my liking, but they were incredibly atmospheric. Good writing overall.

One reference that stuck out in my head:

A dark world, full of whispers and a man with a wide grin.

Nope nope nope. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You mean the recovered documents? I suppose I could limit them! Thanks for the input

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am very glad to see this back. It's an original idea, with a wonderful atmosphere and a solid buildup/progression that work hand-in-hand to immerse one in the world you've crafted. This is only undone by the over-explaining via endnotes trope - which is hurting more than helping. I would look into ways the information contained there could be scattered into the explorations. For now, neutral vote.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Picture came from pintrest, from someone who took it from a Russian site here hitrostigizni.ru, but the image was once again taken from another website. I think this is okay

Images used in posted articles must comply with the site's image policy (e.g. Creative Commons, Public Domain). A source must be provided on the discussion page and the source must be compatible with the site's license. The author will be notified after this posting via Wikidot PM and will have 48 hours to properly source the image before it is edited out of the page. Please post the images source as a reply to this post.

EDIT: Image removed since proper attribution cannot be fully provided.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I submitted it as a 3000, knowing it probably wouldn't make it. I didn't notice there were still series 3 slots that weren't taken, since they are few and far between at this point.

The image does concern me, since it is seemingly a gray area with this image in particular

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So if I remember correctly, this was a 3000 entry. While I didn't think it fit the 3000 spot, it fits much better as a standalone skip. About the image, I'm not sure if your breaking copyright rules here, I'd let a more experienced member check that out. Overall, the added logs are an improvement on the 3000 entry.

I think I need to give it another read, until then I'm at a neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: just did ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You left SCP-XXXX in there. Replace it with 2853.

Otherwise I like it :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
hope you like this one!

Referenced a skip and a tale, see if you can spot em

Picture came from pintrest, from someone who took it from a Russian site here hitrostigizni.ru, but the image was once again taken from another website. I think this is okay

Edit: it isn't

Also, fun fact: My headcanon places the building outside of Gravity Falls, Oregon ;)

New pic is up, creative commons

https://www.flickr.com/photos/olavxo/15546925380/

https://www.flickr.com/photos/usdagov/8191524030/

A great thanks to M Element for translating From Dust to Dawn into Chinese! So happy that people enjoy these articles enough to put the effort into translating them.
